expert_analysis.why: WHY: The chaos shred (Image 3) and pitch track (Image 4) perfectly match the rollercoaster baseline for human vocal cords breaking under massive adrenaline. This is not mechanical noise.
expert_analysis.proof: PROOF: The 21% Roughness score alongside a 16.32s delayed high-frequency shriek proves this is biological distress reacting to a previous event. No party yell or door slam produces this sustained, jagged spectral density.

laion_clap.description: Semantic audio-text similarity analysis using 630K+ audio-text pairs

top_matches.description: weird loud non-slam non-bang non-yell sound at 109 seconds
faint talking or yelling from 110 to 120 seconds
faint male voice in pain around 90 seconds
faint whimpering and fast yelling or whimper spikes at 96 to 98 seconds

key_findings: Peak match at 109 seconds: 'weird loud non-slam non-bang non-yell sound' (0.35 cosine similarity)
Temporal correlation: Pain vocalizations detected at 90s, whimpering at 96-98s
Multiple distress signatures: Heatmap shows sustained chaotic/distressed human voices
Cross-validation: LAION-CLAP confirms VGGish acoustic novelty peaks align with semantic distress descriptions

expert_analysis.why: WHY: A massive physical thud occurs at 12.0s, followed exactly 1.44 seconds later by an unprecedented 28.15% Roughness peak (vocal shredding).
expert_analysis.proof: PROOF: The AI Novelty detection maxed out at 0.83 exactly on this spike. This sequence mathematically confirms a violent impact immediately resulting in severe human distress. The visual mess on the spectrogram is unmistakable.

expert_analysis.why: WHY: Sustained high-pitch vocalizations reaching near 2000Hz (Image 4) following a massive Crest Factor physical impact. The vocal cords are pushed beyond conversational limits.
expert_analysis.proof: PROOF: A chaotic impact registers first, followed by a male/female screeched response driving a 24.7% initial Roughness that drops into sustained high-pitch shrieks. The AI confirms this as an acoustic anomaly consistent with profound fear.

expert_analysis.why: WHY: A staggering 21.05 dB Crest Factor spike with very low Roughness (12%). The energy is sharp, fast, and vertically aligned rather than sustained across frequencies.
expert_analysis.proof: PROOF: This is the acoustic signature of a heavy structure being struck, a door slamming violently, or a comparable abrupt physical force—not a human scream. It serves as the physical catalyst for the distress events around it.

expert_analysis.why: WHY: The shred score spikes back to 23.72%, indicating another extreme instance of vocal alarm or chaotic physical violence.
expert_analysis.proof: PROOF: The brick test (Image 1) shows a 16.22 dB spike, while the mess test (Image 2) shows violent broad-spectrum energy. This marks the culmination of the 2:42 sequence in chaos.
